text:
SMART CITY TRAFFIC PERFORMANCE REPORT 2026 
Prepared By: Urban Mobility Analytics Division 
Published: January 2026 
 
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
 
This report evaluates traffic performance across major corridors in the city during the 2025 
calendar year. The study focuses on congestion levels, travel times, accident rates, and 
intelligent transportation system deployments. 
Key findings include: 
• Average city-wide traffic volume increased by 12%. 
• Peak-hour congestion increased by 8%. 
• Travel times increased by 6% in central business districts. 
• AI-based adaptive traffic signal systems reduced delays by up to 18%. 
 
2. TRAFFIC VOLUME ANALYSIS 
 
Daily traffic counts were collected using roadside sensors and camera systems. 
Table 1: Average Daily Vehicle Counts 
Central Avenue: 48,500 vehicles/day 
Metro Avenue: 52,200 vehicles/day 
Ring Road: 64,800 vehicles/day 
Airport Corridor: 38,900 vehicles/day 
Morning Peak Period: 
07:00 AM – 09:00 AM 
Evening Peak Period:

Morning Peak Period: 
07:00 AM – 09:00 AM 
Evening Peak Period: 
05:00 PM – 07:00 PM 
The highest peak-hour volume was recorded on Ring Road with approximately 6,400 
vehicles per hour. 
 
3. CONGESTION HOTSPOTS 
 
The following intersections experienced severe congestion: 
A. Metro Ave & 5th Street 
Level of Service: E 
Average Vehicle Delay: 
95 seconds per vehicle 
Queue Length: 
420 meters during evening peak 
Primary Cause: 
Commercial development and increased pedestrian crossings. 
B. Central Ave & Park Junction 
Level of Service: D 
Average Vehicle Delay: 
62 seconds per vehicle 
Queue Length: 
280 meters 
Primary Cause: 
High bus volumes and frequent curbside parking activity. 
 
4. ROAD SAFETY ANALYSIS 
 
A total of 2,184 traffic incidents were reported during 2025. 
Severity Breakdown: 
Minor Incidents: 
1,624 
Major Incidents: 
486 
Fatal Incidents: 
74 
The majority of incidents occurred between 06:00 PM and 09:00 PM. 
Top Accident Locations: 
1. Ring Road Exit 7

Top Accident Locations: 
1. Ring Road Exit 7 
2. Metro Ave & 5th Street 
3. Airport Corridor Interchange 
 
5. INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION SYSTEMS 
 
Several smart transportation initiatives were deployed. 
Adaptive Traffic Signal Control (ATSC) 
Deployment Locations: 
• Metro Avenue 
• Central Avenue 
• Ring Road 
Results: 
Average Delay Reduction: 
18% 
Average Travel Time Reduction: 
11% 
Fuel Consumption Reduction: 
7% 
The ATSC system uses AI-based prediction models to dynamically adjust signal timing 
based on real-time traffic demand. 
 
6. PUBLIC TRANSPORT PERFORMANCE 
 
Bus Operations: 
Average Daily Ridership: 
412,000 passengers 
Average Bus Speed: 
24 km/h 
On-Time Performance: 
87% 
Metro Operations: 
Average Daily Ridership: 
610,000 passengers 
On-Time Performance: 
94% 
 
7. RECOMMENDATIONS 
 
1. Expand adaptive signal control to all major corridors. 
2. Introduce AI-powered congestion prediction systems. 
3. Upgrade roadside camera infrastructure.

2. Introduce AI-powered congestion prediction systems. 
3. Upgrade roadside camera infrastructure. 
4. Improve pedestrian crossing management at high-volume intersections. 
5. Increase public transport capacity during peak periods. 
 
8. CONCLUSION 
 
The city continues to experience growing transportation demand. Intelligent transportation 
technologies have demonstrated measurable improvements in traffic flow and safety. 
Continued investment in AI-driven traffic management is recommended to support future 
mobility needs.
